title: Extreme Heat and Wildfires Create Global Emergency
subtitle: Record-breaking temperatures in the United States and massive forest fires across Southern Europe are testing public safety and emergency services.

summary.en: A dangerous heatwave in the United States has led to at least 25 deaths and triggered widespread health alerts. Meanwhile, southern Europe is facing a series of intense wildfires that have forced thousands of people to evacuate their homes. Firefighters in France, Portugal, Greece, and Spain are working around the clock to control the flames. Officials warn that these extreme weather events are becoming more frequent and severe. Governments are now increasing cooperation to manage the growing risk to citizens and infrastructure.
summary.zh: 美國的一場危險熱浪已導致至少二十五人死亡，並引發廣泛的健康警報。與此同時，南歐正面臨一系列猛烈的野火，迫使數千人撤離家園。法國、葡萄牙、希臘與西班牙的消防員正日以繼夜地撲滅火勢。官員警告，這些極端天氣事件正變得愈發頻繁且嚴重。各國政府目前正加強合作，以應對對公民與基礎設施日益增長的風險。

paragraphs.en: A dangerous combination of record-breaking heat and out-of-control wildfires is currently affecting millions of people across the United States and southern Europe. As temperatures climb to life-threatening levels, emergency services are struggling to manage the human cost of this extreme weather.
paragraphs.zh: 破紀錄的高溫與失控的野火正形成危險的組合，目前影響著美國與南歐數百萬民眾。隨著氣溫攀升至威脅生命的程度，緊急救難服務單位正竭力應對這些極端天氣所造成的人命損失。

paragraphs.en: In the United States, a massive "heat dome"—a high-pressure area that traps hot air like a lid on a pot—has settled over the eastern half of the country. According to The Guardian, at least 25 people have died due to heat-related causes. New Jersey has been particularly hard hit, with officials reporting 22 deaths across 10 counties. Many of the victims were found in homes without air conditioning or in parked cars. The New Jersey department of public health warned that this is not a typical summer heatwave, noting that the extreme temperatures are dangerous for both humans and animals. Across the country, more than 140 million people were under active heat alerts as of Sunday.
paragraphs.zh: 在美國，一個巨大的「熱穹」——即一種將熱空氣像鍋蓋般困住的高壓區域——已籠罩在該國東半部上空。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，至少已有二十五人因高溫相關原因死亡。紐澤西州受創尤為嚴重，官員指出該州十個郡共傳出二十二起死亡案例。許多受害者被發現時，身處沒有空調的家中或停放的車內。紐澤西州公共衛生部門警告，這並非典型的夏季熱浪，並指出極端高溫對人類與動物皆構成危險。截至週日，全美有超過一億四千萬人處於高溫警報範圍內。

paragraphs.en: While the United States deals with the heat, southern Europe is fighting a different battle. Wildfires are currently burning through forests in France, Portugal, Greece, and Spain. These fires have forced thousands of residents to leave their homes and have caused significant damage to the environment. In France, about 10,000 people were evacuated near the Spanish border after a fire burned more than 1,500 hectares of land, as reported by Al Jazeera. Deutsche Welle noted that firefighters in France are also concerned about the impact on the famous Tour de France bicycle race, as fires near the route could force officials to cancel parts of the event.
paragraphs.zh: 在美國應對高溫的同時，南歐正面臨另一場戰役。法國、葡萄牙、希臘與西班牙的森林目前正遭受野火肆虐。這些火災迫使數千名居民撤離家園，並對環境造成嚴重破壞。根據半島電視台（Al Jazeera）報導，在法國，由於一場火災燒毀超過一千五百公頃土地，靠近西班牙邊境約有一萬人被迫撤離。德國之聲（Deutsche Welle）指出，法國消防員也對著名的環法自由車賽（Tour de France）受到的影響感到擔憂，因為賽道附近的火勢可能迫使主辦單位取消部分賽事。

paragraphs.en: In Portugal, the situation is equally serious. A wildfire that began on Thursday has destroyed 12,000 hectares of land in the central part of the country. According to Deutsche Welle, more than 1,200 firefighters, supported by nearly 400 vehicles and 15 aircraft, have been working to contain the blaze. To help manage the crisis, Spain and Italy have sent extra firefighting teams and water-bombing aircraft to assist their neighbor. This level of international cooperation is becoming more common as countries work together to fight larger and more frequent fires.
paragraphs.zh: 在葡萄牙，情況同樣嚴峻。一場週四爆發的野火已摧毀該國中部一萬兩千公頃的土地。根據德國之聲報導，超過一千兩百名消防員在近四百輛車輛與十五架飛機的支援下，正致力於控制火勢。為了協助處理這場危機，西班牙與義大利已派遣額外的消防隊與滅火飛機前往支援鄰國。隨著各國共同合作以對抗規模更大、頻率更高的火災，這種國際合作層級正變得日益普遍。

paragraphs.en: Spain is also facing its own challenges. In the northeastern Girona region, a fire has ruined nearly 2,200 hectares of land. Local media reported that nearly 50,000 people were told to stay indoors or leave their homes for safety. Authorities arrested one man who is suspected of starting the fire accidentally while using a tool on the side of the road. Similarly, in Greece, a large fire broke out near Athens, forcing officials to deploy more than 150 firefighters and 22 aircraft. In the city of Thessaloniki, residents were ordered to stay inside with their windows closed after a recycling plant fire created toxic smoke.
paragraphs.zh: 西班牙也面臨自身的挑戰。在東北部的赫羅納（Girona）地區，一場火災已摧毀近兩千兩百公頃土地。當地媒體報導，近五萬人被告知應待在室內或撤離家園以策安全。當局逮捕了一名男子，他涉嫌在路邊使用工具時不慎引發火災。同樣地，在希臘，雅典附近爆發了一場大火，迫使官員部署超過一百五十名消防員與二十二架飛機。在塞薩洛尼基（Thessaloniki）市，由於回收廠火災產生有毒煙霧，居民被要求待在室內並關閉窗戶。

paragraphs.en: Experts and officials agree that these events are part of a worrying trend. As the summer season moves into its busiest period, European policymakers have warned that July and August will bring an extreme danger of wildfires. The fires are lasting longer and burning larger areas than in previous years. The National Weather Service in the United States has offered some hope, stating that cooler air from the north should help lower temperatures in the coming days. However, the overall situation remains fragile.
paragraphs.zh: 專家與官員一致認為，這些事件是令人擔憂的趨勢之一。隨著夏季進入最繁忙的時期，歐洲決策者警告，七月與八月將帶來極高的野火風險。這些火災持續的時間比往年更長，燒毀的面積也更大。美國國家氣象局（National Weather Service）則提供了一些希望，表示來自北方的冷空氣應有助於在未來幾天降低氣溫。然而，整體情況依然脆弱。

paragraphs.en: For many, the impact of these disasters is personal and tragic. In Mississippi, a 74-year-old man named Mitchell Ray Cooley died after being exposed to the heat, and an 83-year-old woman named Martha Irene Van Egmond died after falling in her garden during the high temperatures. These stories highlight the urgent need for better public awareness and support for vulnerable people, especially the elderly, during extreme weather events. As governments look toward the future, the focus is shifting to how communities can better prepare for a world where such extreme heat and fire risks are becoming the new normal.
paragraphs.zh: 對許多人而言，這些災難帶來的影響是個人且悲劇性的。在密西西比州，一名七十四歲的男子米契爾·雷·庫利（Mitchell Ray Cooley）因暴露在高溫下而死亡，一名八十三歲的婦女瑪莎·艾琳·范·艾格蒙（Martha Irene Van Egmond）則在高溫期間於花園跌倒後身亡。這些故事凸顯了在極端天氣事件中，提升公眾意識並為弱勢族群（特別是長者）提供支援的迫切需求。隨著各國政府展望未來，焦點正轉向社區該如何做好準備，以應對這種極端高溫與火災風險已成為新常態的世界。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Severely affected by a negative event or situation.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 受到嚴重打擊的；受災嚴重的。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡的 hit 是過去分詞，形容受災程度。文中：New Jersey has been particularly hard hit, with officials reporting 22 deaths across 10 counties.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To keep something harmful under control or within limits.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 控制（火勢、疫情等），防止擴散。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見意思是「包含」，但在新聞中常用於火災或疫情。文中：According to Deutsche Welle, more than 1,200 firefighters, supported by nearly 400 vehicles and 15 aircraft, have been working to contain the blaze.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To start suddenly (used for unpleasant things like fires, wars, or diseases).
keyTerms.definitionZh: （火災、戰爭、疾病等）突然發生；爆發。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指火災突然發生，而非「打破」。文中：Similarly, in Greece, a large fire broke out near Athens, forcing officials to deploy more than 150 firefighters and 22 aircraft.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A usual or expected state or condition.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作形容詞（正常的），這裡作名詞使用。文中：As governments look toward the future, the focus is shifting to how communities can better prepare for a world where such extreme heat and fire risks are becoming the new normal.

sources.name: The Guardian Environment
sources.title: At least 25 people die in US as record heatwave scorches swaths of country

sources.name: Al Jazeera English
sources.title: 10,000 people evacuated as wildfire tears through southern France

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: Wildfires rage in Portugal, Greece, France and Spain

previousArticles.title: As Dangerous Heat Waves Spread, Experts Warn of Growing Health Risks
previousArticles.summaryEn: Extreme heat waves are becoming more frequent and intense across the globe due to climate change. Recent reports from the U.S. and Europe highlight the serious health dangers posed by high temperatures. Experts warn that humidity makes these conditions particularly difficult for the human body to manage. Vulnerable groups, including the elderly and those with existing health conditions, face the highest risks. Staying hydrated and slowly getting used to the heat are essential steps for public safety.

previousArticles.title: Why Hot Nights Are Making Current Heat Waves More Dangerous
previousArticles.summaryEn: Dangerous heat waves are currently affecting large parts of the United States, with temperatures staying high even after the sun goes down. Experts warn that this lack of nighttime cooling prevents the body from recovering from daytime heat. This trend is linked to climate change, which is causing nights to warm faster than days. People without air conditioning are at the highest risk for serious health issues like heat exhaustion. The combination of high heat and humidity makes it difficult for the body to cool itself through sweating.

quiz.question: According to the report, what is the specific reason for the high number of heat-related deaths in New Jersey?

options.A: A lack of emergency services in rural counties.
options.B: The victims were found in places lacking air conditioning or inside parked cars.
options.C: The heat dome caused power outages that disabled cooling systems.
options.D: The elderly population was not warned about the danger in time.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第二段明確指出，新澤西州許多死者是在沒有空調的家中或停放的汽車內被發現的。選項 A 文章未提及；選項 C 雖然提到熱穹頂（heat dome），但並未提及電力中斷；選項 D 雖然提到老年人脆弱，但並未說是因為未收到警告而死亡，故 B 最為精確。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between European countries regarding the current wildfires?

options.A: They are competing with each other to secure limited firefighting resources.
options.B: They are blaming each other for the environmental damage caused by the fires.
options.C: They are increasingly relying on mutual assistance to manage large-scale disasters.
options.D: They have failed to coordinate their efforts, leading to more frequent fires.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段內容，西班牙和義大利派遣了額外的消防隊和飛機協助葡萄牙，且文章提到這種國際合作正變得越來越普遍，顯示各國傾向於透過互助來應對大規模災害。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 B 文中未提及；選項 D 錯誤，因為文章強調的是合作的增加而非失敗。

quiz.question: In the phrase 'a high-pressure area that traps hot air like a lid on a pot', what does the word 'traps' mean?

options.A: To capture or prevent something from escaping.
options.B: To trick someone into a dangerous situation.
options.C: To create a physical barrier using a mechanical device.
options.D: To measure the temperature of a confined space.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此語境中，traps 形容熱空氣被高氣壓區「困住」或「無法散逸」，就像鍋蓋蓋住鍋子一樣。選項 B 是指欺騙；選項 C 雖然提到物理障礙，但 traps 在此處是動詞描述狀態而非製造工具；選項 D 與測量無關。故 A 是最貼切的詞義。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article?

options.A: The Tour de France is likely to be cancelled due to extreme weather conditions.
options.B: The United States and Europe are experiencing record-breaking temperatures this summer.
options.C: Extreme weather events are becoming more frequent and require better preparation for the future.
options.D: International firefighting cooperation is the only way to stop wildfires in Europe.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章從美國的熱浪與歐洲的野火出發，最終在第六與第七段總結出這是一種令人擔憂的趨勢，且政府與社區必須針對這種「新常態」做好準備。選項 A 只是文中一個細節；選項 B 涵蓋範圍太窄，未包含對未來的反思；選項 D 過度誇大，文章僅將合作視為應對手段之一，而非唯一解法。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
